editableNewDialogue.text: Meh, can you do something different—like a shorter scene instead of a whole episode?

Sure—here’s a quick, punchy scene.

INT. CHUM BUCKET - NIGHT

The Chum Bucket is empty except for PLANKTON, who’s set up a tiny stage and a spotlight. A banner reads: “OPEN MIC NIGHT (PLEASE ATTEND).”

PLANKTON
(into microphone)
Welcome, future loyal customers, to the first annual—
(looks around)
—audience-free Open Mic Night.

The door CREAKS open. SQUIDWARD steps in, holding an umbrella like he’s been personally offended by the weather.

SQUIDWARD
I came in here to avoid a marching band. Somehow I’ve made a worse decision.

PLANKTON
Squidward! A living attendee! Quick—clap for me.

SQUIDWARD
No.

PLANKTON
Fine. I’ll clap for me.

Plankton claps. It’s painfully loud in the empty room.

From outside, SPONGEBOB’s face presses against the window like a cheerful sticker.

SPONGEBOB
(muffled)
Is this… art?

The door swings open again and MR. KRABS walks in, sniffing the air like money has a scent.

MR. KRABS
I heard the words “open” and “night.” Both sound profitable.

PLANKTON
It’s open mic night! I’m going to win hearts with comedy, and while they’re laughing—
(pauses)
—I’ll sell them chum.

SQUIDWARD
That might be the first joke you’ve told.

PLANKTON
Thank you. Now: observational humor. Ever notice how nobody comes in here?

Silence.

SPONGEBOB
(raising hand)
I noticed!

PLANKTON
Great! You’re my target demographic: “person who notices things.” Now laugh.

SPONGEBOB
(trying)
Ha… ha…
(earnestly)
I can do better! Give me a warm-up!

MR. KRABS
I’ll warm up me wallet if there’s a cover charge.

Squidward rolls his eyes.

SQUIDWARD
Fine. I’ll do one poem, and then I’m leaving.

PLANKTON
Yes! Classy! This place desperately needs class.

SQUIDWARD
(reading dramatically)
“An empty room. A lonely mic.
A tiny villain on a spike.
The world is cruel, the lighting’s dim.
And somehow it’s all… about him.”

Plankton wipes away a tear.

PLANKTON
That was beautiful.
(beat)
And also legally mine now.

MR. KRABS
If it’s legally yours, can I sell it?

SPONGEBOB
We could make it a jingle!

All three turn to SpongeBob.

PLANKTON
No.

SQUIDWARD
No.

MR. KRABS
How much?

Plankton lunges at the mic.

PLANKTON
NEW PLAN! The Chum Bucket will become… a poetry restaurant!

SQUIDWARD
That’s still a restaurant.

PLANKTON
Exactly!

SpongeBob gasps.

SPONGEBOB
Wait—does that mean… poems about food?

Mr. Krabs smiles.

MR. KRABS
Poems about payin’.

Plankton stares at the empty dining area, imagining it filled.

PLANKTON
(softly)
…I might actually get one customer.

From outside, a RANDOM FISH passes by and reads the banner.

RANDOM FISH
Open mic night?
(peeks in)
Nope.

They keep walking.

Plankton slumps.

PLANKTON
Audience-free forever.

SpongeBob hops up.

SPONGEBOB
Don’t worry, Plankton! I’ll be your audience! I can clap in different styles!

SpongeBob starts rapid-fire clapping: slow clap, jazz hands, rhythmic applause, off-beat applause.

SQUIDWARD
That’s not different styles. That’s just… chaos.

MR. KRABS
(clapping once)
That one sounded expensive. I like it.

Plankton straightens up, determined.

PLANKTON
Then it’s settled. Open mic night continues until I’m famous—or until the building collapses.

The LIGHT FLICKERS ominously.

CUT TO BLACK.
Can you make it even shorter—like 30 seconds of dialogue?

INT. CHUM BUCKET - NIGHT

PLANKTON
Welcome to Open Mic Night! Attendance is… perfect.

SQUIDWARD
I’m only here because the Krusty Krab is too loud.

MR. KRABS
Is there a fee for bein’ here quietly?

SPONGEBOB
I brought supportive clapping!

PLANKTON
Great. My first joke: Why did the customer cross the road?

SQUIDWARD
To avoid this place.

PLANKTON
Correct.

MR. KRABS
How much for that joke? I can sell it.

PLANKTON
No!

SPONGEBOB
I’ll laugh anyway! HA!

PLANKTON
Finally. A paying customer.

SPONGEBOB
Paying?

PLANKTON
…In spirit.
Can you keep it kinda short this time—like a quick little scene instead of a full episode?

Absolutely—here’s a quick mini-scene with Plankton, Squidward, Mr. Krabs, and SpongeBob.

INT. KRUSTY KRAB - DAY

Mr. Krabs sets up a tiny stage. SpongeBob bounces in place, Squidward looks horrified, and Plankton’s pretending he isn’t interested.

MR. KRABS
Alright, ya barnacles—fast and cheap entertainment!

SQUIDWARD
Of course. Culture… on a budget.

PLANKTON
(quietly)
A “budget” show? Perfect cover for… research.

SPONGEBOB
Don’t worry, everyone! It’ll be a short one—quick, fun, and over before Squidward can complain twice!

Squidward immediately raises a hand like he has a complaint queued up.

FADE OUT.
Actually—quick correction. I don’t really like Plankton. He kinda annoys me. I’m more into Squidward and Mr. Krabs… and SpongeBob’s fine.
Got it—so not Plankton. You prefer Squidward and Mr. Krabs, and SpongeBob is okay. I’ll keep Plankton out of the spotlight next time and center the story on the other three.
